sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.57 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: A group$G$is said to be (conjugacy,$p$-con\-ju\-gacy\/) biprimitively finite\/ if, for any finite subgroup$H$, any two elements of prime order (any two conjugate elements of prime order, of prime order$p$) in$N_G(H)/H$generate a finite subgroup. Do the elements of finite order in a (conjugacy) biprimitively finite group$G$form a subgroup (the periodic part of$G$)? { V.\,P.\,Shunkov} Not always (A....
